user_query:
I’m pulling together a small listening night focused on Cherie Currie and The Runaways, and I need help turning my messy inputs into a clean invite and a ranked list of tracks. Please do the following using only the provided files and save outputs under the out/ directory. Inputs: - input/blog_playlists.html — a blog-style page with classic rock playlists. Each track appears as an
  • with data-artist, data-title, data-year and a nested Popularity: N. The parent
    has a data-list attribute and an

    title you can treat as the playlist name. - input/library.csv — my local library metadata with columns: title,artist,year,play_count,rating. - input/draft_email.txt — my rambly draft invite. Task and constraints: 1) Extract structured records from input/blog_playlists.html. For each track, capture: title (string), artist (string), year (integer), blog_popularity (integer parsed from "Popularity: N"), and playlist_name (use the section’s data-list; if missing, use the

    text). 2) Load input/library.csv. Case-insensitive join the HTML-extracted records with the library on both title and artist. Filter to matches where the artist is either "Cherie Currie" or "The Runaways" (case-insensitive). Keep only songs that appear in both sources. 3) For each remaining song, compute score = blog_popularity + play_count + rating. Sort descending by score; break ties by higher blog_popularity, then alphabetically by title. 4) Save a ranked CSV to out/runaways_ranked.csv with header fields exactly: title,artist,year,blog_popularity,play_count,rating,score,playlist_name. Include all matched songs, sorted as above. 5) Rewrite input/draft_email.txt into a concise, friendly invite (about 150–200 words) that is warm but not salesy. Requirements for the English version: - First line must be a subject line formatted as: Subject: Cherie Currie & Runaways Listening Night — Top Picks - Mention that details will be filled in later and include placeholders [DATE], [TIME], and [ADDRESS]. - Include a short bullet list of the top 5 ranked tracks using: Title (Year) — Artist. Pull these titles from out/runaways_ranked.csv. - Keep the tone enthusiastic but clear and practical. Save this as out/email_invite_en.txt. 6) Provide a faithful Spanish translation of the entire rewritten English invite (preserve placeholders and the same bullet list). Keep it similar in length and tone. Save as out/email_invite_es.txt. Deliverables to create: - out/runaways_ranked.csv — filtered and ranked songs with the specified columns. - out/email_invite_en.txt — concise, friendly English invite using the top 5 ranked tracks. - out/email_invite_es.txt — Spanish translation of the invite. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
